content_blocks.text:
92 
chapter 4
Fast Times in Stim-City
 When Buchanan jammed his twitch-gun into Jai’5 side, Jai froze. Jai knew that Buchanan 
was as stupid as he was ugly and he didn’t want to spook the big guy. Buchanan ordered Jai 
into his boss’5 office, but Jai’d done nothing wrong, (lately), 50 he figured a little chat with 
Buchanan’5 boss Leveler couldn’t be too bad. He’d been moving lots of neural-stimmers in 
the west side lately and he figured Leveler would be pleased. Black market stimmers weren’t 
the best money pump around, but they were pretty harmless. Most of the stim-junkies he’d 
seen tapped out after a while and got back to life, maybe just a little less focused than before.
 Leveler’5 ‘office’ was a skungy looking skimmer, but once Buchanan shoved him in, Jai 
could see that it’d been modified to provide all the extra speed and armor that a local boss like 
Leveler could hope for. “Jai my boy”, hissed Leveler, “pleasure to see you again”. “Likewise 
1’m sure...”, said Jai, sensing the malice behind Leveler’5 greeting, “We should be square 
Leveler, have 1 missed something?” “Ha! You’re making it look pretty good Jai, your volume 
is up, but 1’ve been experiencing, shall we say, a little ‘breach’ lately...” said Leveler. 
 Jai winced involuntarily, he’d been a top drawer jack-hacker in his day. Anytime someone 
figured out how to break a street-jack’5 security, unwanted attention turned toward Jai. “No 
way it’5 me man”, said Jai, “not worth the downside. 1’m retired from hacking, 1 just move 
my stuff and mind my own business”. “Yeah, yeah”, laughed Leveler, “1’m sure you’re 
clean on this one, but 1’11 be losing big margins until this new jack-hacker is shut 
out!” “Well, best of luck Leveler, maybe you could just drop me here and 1’11 go 
move a few more ‘units’ for you before 1 wrap up today”, said Jai. 
 “1’m afraid it’5 not that easy Jai, Buchanan here tells me that word is you’re 
current on J37NE”, insinuated Leveler. “Neural Edition? sure 1 play around a bit, 50 
what?”, Jai responded feeling a little queasy. “Neural edition’5 how 1 let the stim-junkies 
know where the next drop will be”, explained Leveler. “Trouble is, some stim-junkie’5 stayed 
straight long enough to figure out how to hack into my WareHousing database.” “1 need a 
quick thinker like yourself Jai, to take a look at my StimDrop J37NE class; methods, instance 
variables, the whole enchilada, and figure out how they’re getting in. It should...”, “HEY!”, 
exclaimed Buchanan, “1 don’t want no scum hacker like Jai nosin’ around my code!” “Easy 
big guy”, Jai saw his chance, “1’m sure you did a top rate job with your access modi... “Don’t 
tell me - bit twiddler!”, shouted Buchanan, “1 left all of those junkie level methods public, 
50 they could access the drop site data, but 1 marked all the critical WareHousing methods 
private. Nobody on the outside can access those methods buddy, nobody!”
 “1 think 1 can spot your leak Leveler, what say we drop Buchanan here off at the corner 
and take a cruise around the block”, suggested Jai. Buchanan reached for his twitch-gun but 
Leveler’5 stunner was already on Buchanan’5 neck, “Let it go Buchanan”, sneered Leveler, 
“Drop the twitcher and step outside, 1 think Jai and 1 have some plans to make”.
What did Jai suspect? 
Will he get out of Leveler’5 skimmer with all his bones intact?
Five-Minute
Mystery
puzzle: Five Minute Mystery
